docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

ps -ef | grep wait

docs.title: Create a SSH key from WLS
1. Check for Existing Keys
docs.text:
Before creating a new key, check if one already exists in your local WSL environment. 

ls -al ~/.ssh
If this returns "No such file or directory," you do not have an SSH key set up yet.

docs.title: 2. Generate a New SSH Key
docs.text:
Generate a new ED25519 SSH key, replacing the email with your GitHub account email.

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"

Press Enter to accept the default file location and press Enter twice for no passphrase (optional).

docs.title: 3. Add the Key to the SSH Agent
docs.text:
Start the SSH agent in the background and add your newly created private key.

eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

docs.title: 4. Add the Public Key to GitHub
docs.text:
Output your public key to the terminal so you can copy it:

cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
  1. Log in to GitHub and go to Settings > SSH and GPG keys.
  2. Click New SSH key, give it a title (e.g., "WSL Local Machine"), and select Authentication Key.
  3. Paste the copied output and save.

docs.title: 5. Test the Connection
docs.text:
Verify that your WSL environment can successfully authenticate with GitHub.

ssh -T git@github.com
  • If prompted that the authenticity of the host can't be established, type yes and press Enter.
  • A successful connection will return: Hi <username>! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.

docs.title: 6. Troubleshooting: Git Asks for a Password
docs.text:
If Git asks for a username and password when you try to push (e.g., git push -u origin main), it means your local repository was cloned using the HTTPS URL instead of the SSH URL.

To fix this, change the remote URL inside your project folder:

# Ensure you are in your project directory
cd your-project-folder

# Update the remote URL to use SSH
git remote set-url origin git@github.com:<your_username>/<your_repository_name>.git

# Verify the change
git remote -v

# Push your code securely
git push -u origin main

docs.title: Deploying an MkDocs Site from a GitHub Repository to Vercel
docs.text: This guide shows how to deploy an MkDocs project from GitHub to Vercel.

docs.title: 1. Prepare the repository
docs.text:
Make sure your project includes these files:

mkdocs.yml
docs/
pyproject.toml
uv.lock

The mkdocs.yml file controls the site configuration, and the docs/ folder contains the Markdown pages.

docs.title: 2. Add a Vercel configuration
docs.text:
Create a vercel.json file in the root of the repository:

{
  "buildCommand": "uv run mkdocs build",
  "outputDirectory": "site",
  "installCommand": "uv sync --frozen"
}

docs.title: 3. Push the project to GitHub
docs.text:
Commit and push the documentation project:

git status
git add .
git commit -m "Add MkDocs Vercel deployment"
git push

docs.title: 4. Import the repository in Vercel
docs.text:
1. Open Vercel.
  2. Click Add New > Project.
  3. Select the GitHub repository that contains your MkDocs site.
  4. Keep the framework preset as Other.
  5. Confirm these project settings:
Build Command: uv run mkdocs build
Output Directory: site
Install Command: uv sync --frozen

docs.title: 5. Deploy
docs.text: Click Deploy. Vercel will install dependencies, build the MkDocs site, and publish the generated site/ directory.

docs.title: 6. Update the site
docs.text:
After the first deployment, every push to the connected branch will trigger a new Vercel deployment.

git add .
git commit -m "Update docs"
git push

docs.title: Build command fails
docs.text:
Run the same command locally:

uv run mkdocs build

If it fails locally, fix the MkDocs error before redeploying.

docs.title: Vercel cannot find uv
docs.text:
If Vercel does not provide uv in the build environment, replace the Vercel commands with pip-based commands:

{
  "buildCommand": "mkdocs build",
  "outputDirectory": "site",
  "installCommand": "pip install -r requirements.txt"
}

Then add a requirements.txt file:

mkdocs>=1.6.1
mkdocs-material>=9.7.6

docs.title: The deployed site is blank or missing styles
docs.text: Check that outputDirectory is set to site, because MkDocs writes the generated static files there by default.
